adit: noun. A horizontal or nearly horizontal passage from the surface into a mine, as contrasted with a shaft or vertical entry passage, used
air: noun. The substance constituting Earth 's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
art: noun. The conscious production or arrangement of sounds, colours, forms, movements, or other elements in a manner that affects the senses
dah: noun. The spoken representation of a dash in radio and telegraph Morse code.
dart: noun. A pointed missile weapon, intended to be thrown by the hand; for example, a short lance or javelin.
daw: noun. A western jackdaw, Coloeus monedula, a passerine bird in the crow family (Corvidae), more commonly called jackdaw.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
draw: verb. Senses relating to exerting force or pulling.
had: verb. simple past and past participle of have
hair: noun. A pigmented filament of keratin which grows from a follicle on the skin of humans and other mammals.
hart: noun. A male deer, especially the male of the red deer after his fifth year.
hat: noun. A covering for the head, often in the approximate form of a cone, dome or cylinder closed at its top end, and sometimes having a brim
haw: interjection. An imitation of laughter, often used to express scorn or disbelief.
rah: interjection. An exclamation of support or encouragement.
raid: noun. A quick hostile or predatory incursion or invasion in a battle.
rat: noun. Any of the numerous members of several rodent families that usually have short limbs, a pointy snout, a long, hairless tail, and a
rath: noun. A walled enclosure, especially in Ireland; a ringfort built sometime between the Iron Age and the Viking Age.
ria: noun. A narrow inlet of the sea formed by a flooded unglaciated river valley.
rid: verb. Followed by of: to free (oneself or someone, or a place) from an annoyance or hindrance.
tad: noun. A small amount; a little bit.
tahr: noun. Any of three genera of large Asian ungulates belonging to the subfamily Caprinae (goat-antelopes).
tar: noun. A black, oil, sticky, viscous substance, consisting mainly of hydrocarbons derived from organic materials such as wood, peat, or coal.
taw: verb. To prepare or dress, as hemp, by beating; to tew.
thaw: verb. To gradually cause (something frozen, such as earth, ice, or snow) to melt or soften by raising the temperature.
third: adjective. The ordinal form of the cardinal number three; Coming after the second.
twa: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Twana.
wadi: noun. A valley, gully, or stream bed in northern Africa and southwest Asia that remains dry except during the rainy season.
wair: noun. A plank six feet long and one foot across.
wait: verb. To delay movement or action until some event or time; to remain neglected or in readiness.
war: noun. Organized, large-scale, armed conflict between countries or between national, ethnic, or other sizeable groups, usually but not
ward: noun. A warden; a guard; a guardian or watchman.
wart: noun. A type of deformed growth occurring on the skin caused by the human papillomavirus (HPV).
wat: noun. A Buddhist temple in Southeast Asia, especially those in Thailand, Laos, and Cambodia.
what: determiner. Which, especially which of an open-ended set of possibilities.
whid: noun. A quick motion; a rapid, quiet movement, usually by small game.
whit: noun. The smallest part or particle imaginable; an iota.
width: noun. The state of being wide.
withdraw: verb. To draw or pull (something) away or back from its original position or situation.
wraith: noun. A ghost or specter, especially a person's likeness seen just after their death.
wrath: noun. Great anger; an instance of this.
writ: noun. A written order, issued by a court, ordering someone to do (or stop doing) something.
adh: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adhola.
ard: noun. A simple plough consisting of a spike dragged through the soil.
awd: symbol. ISO 639-5 language code for Arawakan languages.
dar: noun. A fish found in the Severn River; a dart or dace.
dha: noun. Alternative form of dah (“Burmese knife”).
ida: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Idakho-Isukha-Tiriki.
itd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Tidung.
rtw: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rathawi.
tai: noun. A fish, the Japanese bream.
tari: noun. A small gold coin, worth a quarter of a dinar, struck by the Arabs of Sicily in the 9th century.
tawhid: noun. The central Islamic doctrine of God's divine unity.
tdr: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Todrah.
thd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kuuk Thaayorre.
tra: noun. Synonym of iridescent shark (“a shark catfish of species Pangasianodon hypophthalmus”).
waid: adjective. Oppressed with weight; crushed; weighed down.
wir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Wiraféd.
